whatThisIs: Compute pediatric maintenance IV fluid rate using the Holliday-Segar 4-2-1 rule: 4 mL/kg/hr for the first 10 kg, 2 mL/kg/hr for the next 10 kg, and 1 mL/kg/hr for every kilogram thereafter. Sophie Well takes a weight in kilograms and returns the hourly rate along with the daily volume (the 100-50-20 daily equivalent), so a clinician can prescribe either an hourly drip or a 24-hour total without recomputing.
whenToUse: Use this when prescribing maintenance fluids for an admitted pediatric patient, when checking that an existing order matches the weight on chart, or when a resident wants to confirm a back-of-envelope calculation before the order goes into the EHR. The 4-2-1 rule assumes a euvolemic patient at baseline; bolus replacement, ongoing losses, and dextrose strategy live outside this tool. Modern practice favors isotonic maintenance fluids (per AAP 2018 guidance) over the historical hypotonic default - the rate is the math; the fluid composition is a clinical decision.
